items.question: Which metric should primarily drive checkpoint selection during training?
options: val/f1 or val/loss on held-out data
items.answer: val/f1 or val/loss on held-out data
items.explanation: Validation metrics estimate generalization during development; train metrics overstate fit and test metrics should not be used iteratively.

items.question: What naming pattern best distinguishes metrics by data split?
items.explanation: Split prefixes (train/, val/, test/) make curves comparable and reveal train vs validation divergence.

items.question: Train/loss keeps decreasing but val/loss rises after epoch 20. What does this suggest?
items.explanation: Improving train loss with worsening validation loss is the classic overfitting signature on scalar curves.

items.question: When should test/f1 typically be logged?
options: Once after selecting the best validation checkpoint
items.answer: Once after selecting the best validation checkpoint
items.explanation: Test metrics are for final sign-off— logging them every epoch invites test-set-driven selection.
